memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XRFdc_StatusHandler)(void *CallBackRef, u32 Type, u32 Tile_Id, u32 Block_Id, u32 StatusEvent)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *CallBackRef, u32 Type, u32 Tile_Id, u32 Block_Id, u32 StatusEvent)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_StatusHandler

briefdescription.para: The handler data type allows the user to define a callback function to respond to interrupt events in the system.

detaileddescription.para: This function is executed in interrupt context, so amount of processing should be minimized.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked. Its type is not important to the driver, so it is a void pointer.
indicates ADC/DAC.
indicates Tile number (0-3).
indicates Block number (0-3).
indicates one or more interrupt occurred.

memberdef.argsstring: (metal_phys_addr_t BaseAddr)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_LookupConfig

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, u16 DeviceId, struct metal_device **DevicePtr)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_RegisterMetal

briefdescription.para: Register/open the deviceand map RFDC to the IO region.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
contains the ID of the device to register/map
is a pointer to the metal device.

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, XRFdc_Config *ConfigPtr)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_CfgInitialize

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific XRFdc instance such that the driver is ready to use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about xrfdc. This function initializes an InstancePtr object for a specific device specified by the contents of Config.

simplesect.para: The user needs to first call the XRFdc_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer which is passed as a parameter to the XRFdc_CfgInitialize() API.

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, u32 Type, int Tile_Id)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_StartUp

briefdescription.para: The API Restarts the requested tile.

detaileddescription.para: It can restart a single tile and alternatively can restart all the tiles. Existing register settings are not lost or altered in the process. It just starts the requested tile(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
is ADC or DAC. 0 for ADC and 1 for DAC
Valid values are 0-3, and -1.

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, u32 Type, int Tile_Id)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_Shutdown

briefdescription.para: The API stops the tile as requested.

detaileddescription.para: It can also stop all the tiles if asked for. It does not clear any of the existing register settings. It just stops the requested tile(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
is ADC or DAC. 0 for ADC and 1 for DAC
Valid values are 0-3, and -1.

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, u32 Type, int Tile_Id)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_Reset

briefdescription.para: The API resets the requested tile.

detaileddescription.para: It can reset all the tiles as well. In the process, all existing register settings are cleared and are replaced with the settings initially configured (through the GUI).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
is ADC or DAC. 0 for ADC and 1 for DAC
Valid values are 0-3, and -1.

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, u32 Type, int Tile_Id, u32 StartState, u32 EndState)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_CustomStartUp

briefdescription.para: The API starts the requested tile from a provided state and runs to the given end state.

detaileddescription.para: It can restart a single tile and alternatively can restart all the tiles.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
is ADC or DAC. 0 for ADC and 1 for DAC
Valid values are 0-3, and -1.
Valid values are XRFDC_START_STATE_*.
Valid values are XRFDC_END_STATE_*.

simplesect.para: If starting from/ending at XRFDC_START_STATE_OFF/XRFDC_END_STATE_OFF, register settings will be wiped.

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, u32 Type, u32 Tile_Id, u32 State)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_WaitForState

briefdescription.para: This function is used to wait for a tile to reach a given state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
represents ADC or DAC.
Valid values are 0-3.
represents the state which the tile must reach.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
is ADC or DAC. 0 for ADC and 1 for DAC

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, u32 Type, u32 Tile_Id, u32 Block_Id, XRFdc_Mixer_Settings *MixerSettingsPtr)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_SetMixerSettings

briefdescription.para: The API is used to update various mixer settings, fine, coarse, NCO etc.

detaileddescription.para: Mixer/NCO settings passed are used to update the corresponding block level registers. Driver structure is updated with the new values.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
is ADC or DAC. 0 for ADC and 1 DAC
Valid values are 0-3.
is ADC/DAC block number inside the tile. Valid values are 0-3.
Pointer to the XRFdc_Mixer_Settings structure in which the Mixer/NCO settings are passed.

simplesect.para: FineMixerScale in Mixer_Settings structure can have 3 values. XRFDC_MIXER_SCALE_* represents the valid values. XRFDC_MIXER_SCALE_AUTO - If mixer mode is R2C, Mixer Scale is set to 1 and for other modes mixer scale is set to 0.7 XRFDC_MIXER_SCALE_1P0 - To set fine mixer scale to 1. XRFDC_MIXER_SCALE_0P7 - To set fine mixer scale to 0.7.

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, u32 Type, u32 Tile_Id, u32 Block_Id, XRFdc_Mixer_Settings *MixerSettingsPtr)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_GetMixerSettings

briefdescription.para: The API returns back Mixer/NCO settings to the caller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
is ADC or DAC. 0 for ADC and 1 for DAC
Valid values are 0-3.
is ADC/DAC block number inside the tile. Valid values are 0-3.
Pointer to the XRFdc_Mixer_Settings structure in which the Mixer/NCO settings are passed.

simplesect.para: FineMixerScale in Mixer_Settings structure can have 3 values. XRFDC_MIXER_SCALE_* represents the valid values. XRFDC_MIXER_SCALE_AUTO - If mixer mode is R2C, Mixer Scale is set to 1 and for other modes mixer scale is set to 0.7 XRFDC_MIXER_SCALE_1P0 - To set fine mixer scale to 1. XRFDC_MIXER_SCALE_0P7 - To set fine mixer scale to 0.7.

memberdef.name: XRFdc_GetThresholdSettings

briefdescription.para: Threshold settings are read from the corresponding registers and are passed back to the caller.

detaileddescription.para: There can be two threshold settings: threshold0 and threshold1. Both of them are independent of each other. The function returns the requested threshold (which can be threshold0, threshold1, or both.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
Valid values are 0-3.
is ADC/DAC block number inside the tile. Valid values are 0-3.
Pointer through which the register settings for thresholds are passed back..

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, u32 Tile_Id, u32 Block_Id, XRFdc_Threshold_Settings *ThresholdSettingsPtr)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_SetThresholdSettings

briefdescription.para: Threshold settings are updated into the relevant registers.

detaileddescription.para: Driver structure is updated with the new values. There can be two threshold settings: threshold0 and threshold1. Both of them are independent of each other. The function returns the requested threshold (which can be threshold0, threshold1, or both.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
Valid values are 0-3.
is ADC/DAC block number inside the tile. Valid values are 0-3.
Pointer through which the register settings for thresholds are passed to the API.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.

is DAC block number inside the tile. Valid values are 0-3.
Valid values are 1 (Maximum SNR, for non- randomized decoder), 2 (Maximum Linearity, for randomized decoder)

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 Vector, void *XRFdcPtr)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_IntrHandler

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the driver.

detaileddescription.para: It must be connected to an interrupt system by the application such that it can be called when an interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is interrupt vector number. Libmetal status handler expects two parameters in the handler prototype, hence kept this parameter. This is not used inside the interrupt handler API.
contains a pointer to the driver instance

simplesect.para: Vector param is not useful inside the interrupt handler, hence typecast with void to remove compilation warning.

memberdef.argsstring: (XRFdc *InstancePtr, void *CallBackRef, XRFdc_StatusHandler FunctionPtr)
memberdef.name: XRFdc_SetStatusHandler

briefdescription.para: This function sets the status callback function, the status handler, which the driver calls when it encounters conditions that should be reported to the higher layer software.

detaileddescription.para: The handler executes in an interrupt context, so the amount of processing should be minimized

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRFdc instance.
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
is the pointer to the callback function.

simplesect.para: The handler is called within interrupt context, so it should finish its work quickly.

memberdef.name: XRFdc_DynamicPLLConfig

briefdescription.para: This function used for dynamically switch between internal PLL and external clcok source and configuring the internal PLL.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRfdc instance.
indicates ADC/DAC
indicates Tile number (0-3)
Clock source internal PLL or external clock source
Reference Clock Frequency in MHz(102.40625MHz - 1.2GHz)
Sampling Rate in MHz(0.1- 6.554GHz for DAC and 0.5/1.0 - 2.058/4.116GHz for ADC based on the device package).

simplesect.para: This API enables automatic selection of the VCO which will work in IP version 2.0.1 and above. Using older version of IP this API is not likely to work.

listitem.para:
XRFDC_MTS_TIMEOUT if timeout occurs.

XRFDC_MTS_MARKER_MISM
XRFDC_MTS_NOT_SUPPORTED if MTS is not supported.

memberdef.name: XRFdc_GetDeviceNameByDeviceId

briefdescription.para: Traverse "/sys/bus/platform/device" directory, to find RFDC device entry, corresponding to provided device id.

detaileddescription.para: If device entry corresponding to said device id is found, store it in output buffer DevNamePtr.

parameterdescription.para: is base address of char array, where device name will be stored
contains the ID of the device to look up the RFDC device name entry in "/sys/bus/platform/device"

listitem.para: XRFDC_FAILURE if device entry not found for given device id.
